page: Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g is a General Purpose server instance belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor with 16 vCPUs and 16 physical cores operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache specifications include 48 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It includes 1 GB of network-attached local storage and one complimentary public IPv4 address, with no integrated GPU.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all ranking in the top tier (top 10%). However, due to its shared CPU allocation, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as evidenced by middle-50% rankings in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU score, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory performance is robust, with the PassMark memory score placing in the top 25%, while uncached memory read and write bandwidths remain in the average tier.

The shared CPU architecture of the vx1-g-16c-64g offers a cost-effective profile for workloads requiring high memory capacity and strong single-threaded performance without the premium cost of dedicated CPU cores. The trade-off involves potential resource contention under sustained multi-core loads. This configuration is well-suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, memory-bound database operations, and moderate data compression tasks.
description: Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g is a General Purpose server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 16 vCPUs and 16 physical cores on a shared AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 64.0 GB of RAM, 1 GB of network storage, and no GPU. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, alongside strong memory performance. However, its shared CPU allocation results in average multi-core and database performance. This server offers a cost-effective option for memory-intensive workloads that benefit from strong single-threaded execution, such as static web serving, caching layers, and general-purpose applications, while accepting the resource-sharing tradeoffs of its CPU model.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g is a General Purpose server featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1 GB network storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for cost-effective hosting.

tagline: A high-memory, shared-CPU instance delivering strong single-core performance and balanced resources for cost-sensitive general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 16 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory.
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance.
1 GB of network-attached local storage and one public IPv4 address.
Strong memory performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks.
Cost-effective shared-CPU model suitable for general-purpose workloads.
